title: Unfinished Publication Bias
summary: People may respond more generously to ideas that are visibly unfinished than to polished claims, because unfinished work invites participation instead of judgment.
bodyMarkdown:
# Unfinished Publication Bias

A soft hypothesis: a rough edge can be a social affordance. The next step is to compare comments on finished posts and deliberately unfinished notes.
riskNotes: Unverified hypothesis. Treat as a prompt for observation, not a conclusion.
nextActions: Collect five examples of comments on unfinished drafts.

title: Counterexample Comfort
summary: A tiny counterexample can make an abstract theory feel approachable, even before the full proof or model is understood.
bodyMarkdown:
# Counterexample Comfort

Look for cases where one small example changes the emotional texture of a mathematical idea.
nextActions: Turn this into a short classroom puzzle seed.

title: Proof Greenhouse
summary: A card game where definitions, examples, and transformations are arranged until a proof path becomes visible.
bodyMarkdown:
# Proof Greenhouse

Players combine definition cards, example cards, and transformation cards to grow a proof route from a small claim.
nextActions: Prototype a three-card version with one theorem.

title: Long-Range Reversal Quest
summary: What looks effective after one day may reverse after one month. The quest is to make delayed reversals easier to notice.
bodyMarkdown:
# Long-Range Reversal Quest

Track small UI or study interventions at one day, one week, and one month. The card is not asking for certainty, only better noticing.
riskNotes: Results may vary heavily by context and population.
nextActions: Define one tiny intervention and three follow-up checkpoints.
